grain.desc: Staple crop. Primary source of calories for the population.

fish.desc: Salted or fresh catch. Supplements grain in coastal and riverside settlements.

meat.desc: Butchered livestock or hunted game. Nobles demand it as a staple.

game.desc: Wild animal meat from the forests and plains.

wood.desc: Cut timber. Consumed daily by the population for heat and cooking.

coal.desc: Mined sedimentary fuel. Burns hotter than wood; used in smelting.

peat.desc: Bog fuel. Inferior to wood but available in swampy terrain.

wool.desc: Shorn from sheep. Refined into cloth or sold raw.

cloth.desc: Woven wool or linen. Required for garments and basic population maintenance.

linen.desc: Woven flax. Lighter and finer than wool cloth.

silk.desc: Imported luxury fabric. Only the nobility wears it.

leather.desc: Tanned hide. Used in armor, boots, and general maintenance.

hides.desc: Raw untanned animal hides. Refined into leather.

furs.desc: Pelts from cold-climate animals. A noble luxury and status marker.

fine_garments.desc: Tailored silk and fine cloth garments. Purely a luxury good.

ale.desc: Brewed grain beverage. Burghers demand it; shortage causes burgher unrest.

salt.desc: Preservative and flavouring. Nobles require it; also vital for preservation.

spices.desc: Rare imported flavouring. Only reachable via long-distance trade routes.

ivory.desc: Carved tusks. Purely a prestige commodity.

jewelry.desc: Crafted gold and gem trinkets. Nobility demand scales with their population.

iron.desc: Smelted from iron ore. Backbone of tools, weapons, and armor.

copper.desc: Soft metal from metamorphic rock. Alloyed into bronze.

tin.desc: Soft metal from metamorphic rock. Combined with copper to make bronze.

lead.desc: Dense sedimentary metal. Used in roofing and for crossbow bolts.

silver.desc: Precious metal from metamorphic rock. Used in coinage and fine jewelry.

gold.desc: Rarest igneous metal. Primary source of high-value coinage and jewelry.

bronze.desc: Copper-tin alloy. Used in early-tier weapons and armor.

steel.desc: Refined iron alloy. Required for high-quality weapons and armor.

stone.desc: Quarried rock. Used in wall and building construction.

marble.desc: Metamorphic decorative stone. Used in prestige buildings and cathedrals.

clay.desc: Found near rivers and swamps. Fired into bricks.

sand.desc: Desert or riverbed granule. Melted into glass.

gems.desc: Precious stones from igneous deposits. Used in high-end jewelry.

tools.desc: Iron implements for farming and mining. Increase labor efficiency.

bricks.desc: Fired clay blocks. Alternative to stone for construction.

horses.desc: Trained horses for cavalry and transport. Cannot be carried in a caravan.

livestock.desc: Cattle, pigs, and sheep kept for meat and byproducts.
